behaviors.description: Reviewer dispatch sends the same source document and correct reviewer-specific instruction pack to each reviewer role.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff (dispatcher -> reviewer adapters/config). A unit test of one helper could miss mismatched role pack wiring. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is about request construction/dispatch correctness, not model judgment, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Wire dispatcher with real role-config module and stub reviewer clients; execute one dispatch and assert each reviewer call received identical document bytes plus its own expected instruction pack.

behaviors.description: The live clause extractor identifies key terms (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) present in the contract.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model output quality (extraction accuracy). Stubs would hide failures in what the model actually identifies. Rejected Workflow: this behavior is one reviewer’s decision quality, not multi-step agentic sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real clause extractor on a curated contract set with gold annotations for parties/dates/obligations/penalties; assert recall/precision thresholds and critical-term presence.

behaviors.description: Risk-policy configuration is passed correctly to the risk assessor invocation.
behaviors.justification: This is invocation wiring across modules (policy loader -> request builder -> assessor client). Real model output is not under test. Rejected Agentic/System: behavior text is exclusively about passing configuration, not policy interpretation quality or OS/network effects.
behaviors.test_description: Compose real config parsing + invocation builder with a stub risk-assessor endpoint/client; invoke analysis and assert transmitted payload contains exact policy id/rules/version expected.

behaviors.description: The live risk assessor flags language that is problematic under policy, including indirect or euphemistic phrasing.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets semantic detection quality (including euphemisms), which depends on real model judgment and non-determinism. Mocked responses would trivially pass and miss true failures. Rejected Workflow: single reviewer quality, not chained agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Execute live risk assessor on contracts containing direct and euphemistic risky clauses with human-labeled expectations; assert detection rate and false-negative bounds by policy category.

behaviors.description: Jurisdiction and regulatory profile are passed correctly to the compliance checker invocation.
behaviors.justification: Failure occurs at module boundary (context assembly -> compliance invocation payload). No need for real model semantics to detect wrong/omitted jurisdiction fields. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is parameter passing only, disqualifying Agentic/Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Run orchestrator with real context-building modules and a stub compliance client; assert request contains exact jurisdiction code and regulatory profile mapping expected for the document.

behaviors.description: The live compliance checker correctly determines whether the contract meets all configured regulatory requirements.
behaviors.justification: This is model decision correctness against regulatory criteria; fake outputs would hide misclassification failures. Rejected Workflow: one reviewer’s decision quality, not multi-agent sequential reasoning.
behaviors.test_description: Run live compliance checker against labeled contracts spanning pass/fail regulatory scenarios per jurisdiction profile; assert decision accuracy and critical-requirement violation detection thresholds.

behaviors.description: When two reviewers flag the same clause with conflicting assessments (for example extractor says `standard` but risk assessor says `high risk`), the merge step preserves both assessments with attribution.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is in cross-module schema/merge handoff (reviewer outputs -> merger), not single-model quality. Unit may miss contract-wide wiring/normalization issues between reviewer result formats. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: behavior text concerns deterministic merge conflict handling, not correctness of model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Feed merger through normalizer pipeline with realistic reviewer output fixtures in conflicting cases; assert unified report retains both assessments, source reviewer attribution, and no overwrite/loss.

behaviors.description: End-to-end orchestration (dispatch all three reviewers, collect outputs, merge unified report) succeeds on a real contract without losing any reviewer findings.
behaviors.justification: This chains multiple real agentic steps (three live reviewers) plus orchestration/merge. Failures can emerge only across the sequence (parallel calls, collection, consolidation, loss). Rejected lower levels: behavior text explicitly requires full end-to-end on a real contract.
behaviors.test_description: Run full pipeline on real contracts with live extractor/risk/compliance reviewers in parallel; verify final unified report includes all findings from each reviewer with no dropped items and successful completion metadata.
